title: Komercnoslēpuma aizsardzības likums
description: Latvian law implementing Directive (EU) 2016/943 on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure.

provisions.title: Purpose of the Law
provisions.content: The purpose of this Law is to establish the legal framework for the protection of commercial secrets (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure, implementing Directive (EU) 2016/943 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure.

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law: 1) Commercial secret (trade secret) means information which meets all of the following requirements: a) it is secret in the sense that it is not generally known among or readily accessible to persons within the circles that normally deal with the kind of information in question; b) it has commercial value because it is secret; c) it has been subject to reasonable steps under the circumstances, by the person lawfully in control of the information, to keep it secret; 2) Commercial secret holder means any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a commercial secret; 3) Infringer means any natural or legal person who has unlawfully acquired, used, or disclosed a commercial secret.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II - Lawful and Unlawful Acts
provisions.title: Lawful Acquisition of Commercial Secrets
provisions.content: The acquisition of a commercial secret shall be considered lawful when it is obtained by: a) independent discovery or creation; b) observation, study, disassembly, or testing of a product or object that has been made available to the public or is lawfully in the possession of the acquirer; c) exercise of the right of workers or workers' representatives to information and consultation in accordance with law; d) any other practice which, under the circumstances, is in conformity with honest commercial practices.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II - Lawful and Unlawful Acts
provisions.title: Unlawful Acquisition
provisions.content: The acquisition of a commercial secret without the consent of the commercial secret holder shall be considered unlawful whenever carried out by: a) unauthorised access to, appropriation of, or copying of any documents, objects, materials, substances, or electronic files lawfully under the control of the commercial secret holder containing the commercial secret or from which the commercial secret can be deduced; b) any other conduct which, under the circumstances, is considered contrary to honest commercial practices.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II - Lawful and Unlawful Acts
provisions.title: Unlawful Use and Disclosure
provisions.content: The use or disclosure of a commercial secret shall be considered unlawful whenever carried out, without the consent of the commercial secret holder, by a person who has acquired the commercial secret unlawfully, is in breach of a confidentiality agreement or any other duty not to disclose the commercial secret, or is in breach of a contractual or any other duty to limit the use of the commercial secret.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II - Lawful and Unlawful Acts
provisions.title: Infringing Goods
provisions.content: The production, offering, or placing on the market of infringing goods, or the importation, export, or storage of infringing goods for those purposes, shall also be considered an unlawful use of a commercial secret where the person carrying out such activities knew or ought, under the circumstances, to have known that the commercial secret was used unlawfully. Infringing goods means goods whose design, characteristics, functioning, production process, or marketing significantly benefits from commercial secrets unlawfully acquired, used, or disclosed.

provisions.title: Exceptions to Protection
provisions.content: The application of the measures, procedures, and remedies provided for in this Law shall not restrict the exercise of the right to freedom of expression and information, the right to disclose a wrongdoing, a misconduct, or an illegal activity (whistleblowing), or the disclosure by workers to their trade union representatives in the exercise of their functions. Commercial secret protection shall not prevent competent authorities from accessing commercial secrets when necessary for the performance of their functions.

provisions.title: Whistleblower Protection
provisions.content: The acquisition, use, or disclosure of a commercial secret shall be considered lawful to the extent that it is carried out for revealing a misconduct, wrongdoing, or illegal activity, provided that the person acted for the purpose of protecting the general public interest. This provision shall be interpreted in accordance with Directive (EU) 2019/1937 on the protection of persons who report breaches of Union law.

provisions.title: Interim and Precautionary Measures
provisions.content: The court may, at the request of the commercial secret holder, order interim measures against the alleged infringer, including: a) the cessation of or prohibition from the use or disclosure of the commercial secret on a provisional basis; b) the prohibition of the production, offering, placing on the market, or use of infringing goods; c) the seizure or delivery up of the alleged infringing goods. The court shall consider whether the application is justified and shall require the applicant to provide evidence of ownership of the commercial secret.

provisions.title: Injunctions and Corrective Measures
provisions.content: Where a judicial decision is taken finding the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of a commercial secret, the court may order: a) the cessation of or prohibition from the use or disclosure of the commercial secret; b) the prohibition of the production, offering, placing on the market, or use of infringing goods; c) the destruction of all or part of any document, object, material, substance, or electronic file containing or embodying the commercial secret; d) the recall of infringing goods from the market.

provisions.title: Damages
provisions.content: The infringer who knowingly or with reasonable grounds to know engaged in unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of a commercial secret shall pay the commercial secret holder damages appropriate to the actual prejudice suffered. When setting the damages, the court shall take into account: the negative economic consequences, including lost profits; the unfair profits made by the infringer; and, in appropriate cases, elements other than economic factors, such as the moral prejudice caused. As an alternative, the court may set the damages as a lump sum based on the royalties or fees which would have been due.

provisions.title: Limitation Period
provisions.content: The limitation period for actions seeking the application of the measures, procedures, and remedies provided for in this Law shall be three years from the date on which the applicant became aware or had reason to become aware of the last act of unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of the commercial secret. Proceedings must be commenced within ten years from the date of the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure.

provisions.chapter: Chapter V - Confidentiality in Court Proceedings
provisions.title: Preservation of Confidentiality in Litigation
provisions.content: The court shall take specific measures to preserve the confidentiality of a commercial secret during and after legal proceedings. These measures may include: restricting access to documents containing the commercial secret to a limited number of persons, restricting access to hearings, making available a non-confidential version of the judicial decision. The court shall balance the right to an effective remedy with the need to protect the commercial secret.

provisions.chapter: Chapter V - Confidentiality in Court Proceedings
provisions.title: Penalties for Breach of Confidentiality Orders
provisions.content: A person who breaches a confidentiality order issued by the court in proceedings relating to the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of a commercial secret shall be subject to penalties in accordance with the procedural law. The court may impose fines, order the exclusion of evidence, or take other measures as appropriate to ensure compliance with the confidentiality order.

provisions.chapter: Chapter V - Confidentiality in Court Proceedings
provisions.title: Safeguards Against Abusive Litigation
provisions.content: Where an action for the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of a commercial secret is found to be manifestly unfounded and the applicant has initiated the proceedings abusively or in bad faith, the court may order the applicant to pay damages to the defendant, to publicise the decision, and to pay the costs of the proceedings. These safeguards are intended to prevent the misuse of commercial secret protection to restrict competition or the mobility of workers.

provisions.title: Digital Protection of Commercial Secrets
provisions.content: A commercial secret holder shall be deemed to have taken reasonable steps to keep information secret within the meaning of Article 2 of this Law if, where the information is stored or transmitted electronically, the holder has implemented appropriate technical and organisational cybersecurity measures, including but not limited to: access controls, encryption, audit logging, network segmentation, and employee training on information security.

provisions.title: Cyber Theft of Commercial Secrets
provisions.content: The unlawful acquisition of a commercial secret through cyber means, including hacking, malware, phishing, social engineering, or exploitation of vulnerabilities in information systems, shall be considered an aggravating circumstance for the purposes of determining damages under Article 11 of this Law. Criminal liability for such acts may also apply under Chapter XVII of the Criminal Law.

provisions.title: Relationship with Other Laws
provisions.content: This Law does not affect the application of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation) with regard to the processing of personal data. Where a commercial secret includes personal data, the processing of such data shall comply with both this Law and the applicable data protection legislation. This Law also does not affect the rights and obligations arising from labour law, including the right of employees to use their general experience and skills.

provisions.title: Entry into Force and Transitional Provisions
provisions.content: This Law shall enter into force on 1 April 2019. Commercial secrets that were protected under the Commercial Law or other legislation prior to the entry into force of this Law shall continue to be protected under this Law. Actions and proceedings commenced before the entry into force of this Law shall be concluded in accordance with the legislation that was in force at the time the action or proceeding was commenced.

definitions.definition: Information that is secret, has commercial value because it is secret, and has been subject to reasonable steps to keep it secret.
Any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a commercial secret.
Any natural or legal person who has unlawfully acquired, used, or disclosed a commercial secret.
Goods whose design, characteristics, functioning, production process, or marketing significantly benefits from commercial secrets unlawfully acquired, used, or disclosed.
